raw_summary: Some users in London can get on the internet, others can't. Wireless works for some but not others. No pattern we can identify.
normalized_summary: some users in london can get on the internet, others can't. wireless works for some but not others. no pattern we can identify.
final_root_cause: Multiple concurrent issues: DHCP scope partially exhausted + one AP down. Conflicting signals masking root cause.
final_resolution: Expanded DHCP scope. Replaced failed AP. Both issues independent.

raw_summary: New-York-Midtown — security scanner reports false positives on internal subnet since last month's ACL cleanup. IT team cannot access management systems.
normalized_summary: new-york-midtown — security scanner reports false positives on internal subnet since last month's acl cleanup. it team cannot access management systems.
final_root_cause: ACL cleanup removed allow rule for IT management subnet. Traffic silently dropped.
final_resolution: Restored allow rule for IT management subnet. Tested all management access. ACL cleanup process updated.

raw_summary: Bangalore-Whitefield — new server connected last week but only getting 100Mbps instead of 1Gbps. Physical cable verified correct.
normalized_summary: bangalore-whitefield — new server connected last week but only getting 100mbps instead of 1gbps. physical cable verified correct.
final_root_cause: Switch port hard-coded to 100Mbps full-duplex by deployment team. Server NIC set to auto-negotiate. Speed mismatch.
final_resolution: Set switch port to auto-negotiate. Port now 1Gbps. Deployment procedure updated.

raw_summary: Chicago-Loop — voice quality degraded since last week. Calls choppy and dropping. Data applications fine. No obvious changes scheduled.
normalized_summary: chicago-loop — voice quality degraded since last week. calls choppy and dropping. data applications fine. no obvious changes scheduled.
final_root_cause: QoS policy fell off switch config after IOS upgrade. Voice traffic no longer prioritised.
final_resolution: Re-applied QoS policy. Voice quality restored. Added QoS config to post-upgrade validation checklist.

raw_summary: New York Midtown and Brooklyn both reporting internet and internal access is down. Started exactly at 02:00 AM, which is outside our change window. No changes scheduled tonight.
normalized_summary: new york midtown and brooklyn both reporting internet and internal access is down. started exactly at 02:00 am, which is outside our change window. no changes scheduled tonight.
final_root_cause: Primary WAN circuit failed at carrier PoP during unannounced carrier maintenance. Failover to secondary circuit did not trigger automatically due to missing track object config.
final_resolution: Manually failed over to secondary circuit. Raised P1 ticket with carrier. Added missing track object to prevent recurrence.

raw_summary: Toronto office — network extremely slow. MTR shows TTL exceeded at several hops within our own AS. Started after last night's maintenance.
normalized_summary: toronto office — network extremely slow. mtr shows ttl exceeded at several hops within our own as. started after last night's maintenance.
final_root_cause: Routing loop introduced by incorrect static route added during maintenance window.
final_resolution: Removed incorrect static route. Verified all paths. Change reviewed and rolled back.

raw_summary: One person in Chicago says video calls are bad. Others on same floor say it is fine.
normalized_summary: one person in chicago says video calls are bad. others on same floor say it is fine.
final_root_cause: End-device issue. User laptop WiFi driver outdated causing poor roaming behaviour.
final_resolution: Updated WiFi driver on user device. Performance restored. Not a network incident.

raw_summary: Seattle-Beacon and Seattle-SLU both down for internet. Internal systems partially reachable. Started 5 minutes ago. No changes in window.
normalized_summary: seattle-beacon and seattle-slu both down for internet. internal systems partially reachable. started 5 minutes ago. no changes in window.
final_root_cause: BGP session flap at Seattle aggregation router. Route withdrawal propagated to both offices.
final_resolution: Cleared BGP session. Applied route dampening. Reviewed BGP timer config.

raw_summary: Bangalore-Whitefield — uplink between access and distribution switch went down. Entire wing B offline. Redundant uplink did not take over.
normalized_summary: bangalore-whitefield — uplink between access and distribution switch went down. entire wing b offline. redundant uplink did not take over.
final_root_cause: Port-channel misconfigured. LACP not enabled on one end. Single link in bundle failed. No redundancy.
final_resolution: Enabled LACP on both ends. Bundle reformed. Redundancy verified. Added port-channel monitoring.

raw_summary: Vancouver-Gastown — IoT devices floor 2 all offline. Regular corporate SSID is fine. The IoT SSID is not visible anywhere on that floor.
normalized_summary: vancouver-gastown — iot devices floor 2 all offline. regular corporate ssid is fine. the iot ssid is not visible anywhere on that floor.
final_root_cause: IoT SSID VLAN assignment removed from AP group during maintenance window config cleanup. SSID still exists but not broadcast on floor 2 APs.
final_resolution: Re-added IoT VLAN to AP group. SSID visible. IoT devices reconnected.

raw_summary: Amsterdam office reporting very slow access to US-based resources only. European SaaS tools are fine. Started about an hour ago.
normalized_summary: amsterdam office reporting very slow access to us-based resources only. european saas tools are fine. started about an hour ago.
final_root_cause: MPLS path to AMER region degraded. Traffic re-routing over suboptimal path with high latency.
final_resolution: Carrier restored MPLS path. Short-term mitigation was QoS policy on backup path.

raw_summary: New-York-Midtown — users walking between floors lose WiFi connection and have to manually reconnect. Issue only on 802.11r-enabled SSIDs.
normalized_summary: new-york-midtown — users walking between floors lose wifi connection and have to manually reconnect. issue only on 802.11r-enabled ssids.
final_root_cause: Fast BSS transition (802.11r) misconfigured on inter-floor AP group boundary. Roaming keys not shared correctly between AP groups.
final_resolution: Corrected 802.11r key sharing config across AP groups. Tested roaming on all floors.

raw_summary: Bangalore-Whitefield — nobody can get on the network. Wired and wireless both affected. Completely blocked.
normalized_summary: bangalore-whitefield — nobody can get on the network. wired and wireless both affected. completely blocked.
final_root_cause: Local RADIUS server VM crashed. No failover to central RADIUS configured for this site.
final_resolution: Restarted RADIUS VM. Configured failover to central ISE as backup. Added VM health monitoring.

raw_summary: Singapore office — extremely high latency to all destinations. Traceroute shows packets looping. Internal resources still reachable with high delay.
normalized_summary: singapore office — extremely high latency to all destinations. traceroute shows packets looping. internal resources still reachable with high delay.
final_root_cause: Routing loop introduced by misconfigured static route pushed during change window.
final_resolution: Removed incorrect static route. Verified routing table. Latency returned to baseline.

raw_summary: Amsterdam office — users logging in and getting limited network access only. Can reach some internal resources but not all. Logging out and back in sometimes helps.
normalized_summary: amsterdam office — users logging in and getting limited network access only. can reach some internal resources but not all. logging out and back in sometimes helps.
final_root_cause: Machine authentication failing silently. Users landing in restricted VLAN instead of corporate VLAN. Computer cert renewal broken by AD group policy change.
final_resolution: Renewed machine certs via GPO. Verified ISE machine auth policy. Users moved to correct VLAN.

raw_summary: All London offices reporting users unable to authenticate. Both wired and wireless affected. Issue is widespread. RADIUS server alarms firing.
normalized_summary: all london offices reporting users unable to authenticate. both wired and wireless affected. issue is widespread. radius server alarms firing.
final_root_cause: Primary RADIUS server CPU at 100%. ISE node overloaded after secondary node was removed for maintenance without updating load balancer config.
final_resolution: Restored secondary ISE node. Load balanced RADIUS traffic. Primary CPU returned to normal.

raw_summary: Network issues in Singapore. Users complaining.
normalized_summary: network issues in singapore. users complaining.
final_root_cause: Unknown — insufficient information to diagnose.
final_resolution: Requested: which office, how many users, which applications, wired or wireless, since when.

raw_summary: New-York-Midtown — all guests unable to access the guest WiFi portal. Corporate network working. Guest SSID visible but portal page doesn't load.
normalized_summary: new-york-midtown — all guests unable to access the guest wifi portal. corporate network working. guest ssid visible but portal page doesn't load.
final_root_cause: Guest portal DNS entry broken after DNS change pushed during maintenance. ISE guest portal IP changed but DNS not updated.
final_resolution: Updated DNS record for guest portal. Portal accessible. DNS change process updated.

raw_summary: Toronto office — log timestamps from network devices look wrong. All showing 1 hour behind. Caused issues with log correlation during an incident last week.
normalized_summary: toronto office — log timestamps from network devices look wrong. all showing 1 hour behind. caused issues with log correlation during an incident last week.
final_root_cause: Daylight saving time change not reflected in NTP config. Timezone set incorrectly on primary NTP server.
final_resolution: Corrected timezone on NTP server. All devices re-synced. Log timestamps correct.

raw_summary: Multiple teams in Austin-HQ reporting Zoom and Chime are unusable. Internal tools working. Issue is external-facing only.
normalized_summary: multiple teams in austin-hq reporting zoom and chime are unusable. internal tools working. issue is external-facing only.
final_root_cause: Upstream provider peering issue affecting real-time media traffic. TCP applications unaffected.
final_resolution: Engaged upstream. Traffic re-routed via alternate peer. Resolved by provider within 90 minutes.

raw_summary: Sydney-CBD — authentication slow for all users. Taking 30-45 seconds to connect instead of 2-3 seconds. Eventually connects. Both wired and wireless.
normalized_summary: sydney-cbd — authentication slow for all users. taking 30-45 seconds to connect instead of 2-3 seconds. eventually connects. both wired and wireless.
final_root_cause: WAN latency between Sydney and central RADIUS server increased to 180ms due to circuit degradation. Authentication timing out and retrying.
final_resolution: Deployed local RADIUS replica to Sydney. Auth latency back to 2 seconds. Carrier engaged for circuit.

raw_summary: New-York-Brooklyn floor 4 — new desks deployed yesterday, users cannot get network connectivity at all. Floor 5 is working. Patch panels verified correct.
normalized_summary: new-york-brooklyn floor 4 — new desks deployed yesterday, users cannot get network connectivity at all. floor 5 is working. patch panels verified correct.
final_root_cause: New switch ports for floor 4 expansion not added to correct VLAN during deployment. Ports in default VLAN 1.
final_resolution: Added VLAN to new ports. Users online. Deployment checklist updated.

raw_summary: All five London offices are down simultaneously. No internal or external access. Escalating urgently.
normalized_summary: all five london offices are down simultaneously. no internal or external access. escalating urgently.
final_root_cause: BGP peering at London regional hub dropped. All five offices share this hub.
final_resolution: Reset BGP session. Added monitoring for BGP hold-timer. Reviewed peer authentication.

raw_summary: Network is slow.
normalized_summary: network is slow.
final_root_cause: Unknown — insufficient information provided to classify.
final_resolution: Escalated back to OMC for more details. Required: site, affected users, wired or wireless, timing.

raw_summary: Austin-HQ — after last night's firewall rule update, one team cannot reach the CI/CD pipeline. All other teams fine. Started exactly at 02:30 AM.
normalized_summary: austin-hq — after last night's firewall rule update, one team cannot reach the ci/cd pipeline. all other teams fine. started exactly at 02:30 am.
final_root_cause: ACL change blocked source subnet of engineering team. Rule too specific and missed one /24 block.
final_resolution: Added missing source subnet to ACL rule. CI/CD access restored. ACL change reviewed.

raw_summary: Chicago-Loop — WAN performance slowly degrading over weeks. Now at about 60% of normal throughput. No single failure event.
normalized_summary: chicago-loop — wan performance slowly degrading over weeks. now at about 60% of normal throughput. no single failure event.
final_root_cause: Fiber degradation over time. Optical power levels declining. Connector needs cleaning and replacement.
final_resolution: Carrier replaced fiber jumper. Optical levels restored. Throughput back to 100%.

raw_summary: Dublin-Docklands — IoT devices offline since last night. Cameras, badge readers, and building systems all affected. Regular user network fine.
normalized_summary: dublin-docklands — iot devices offline since last night. cameras, badge readers, and building systems all affected. regular user network fine.
final_root_cause: IoT VLAN pruned from trunk during overnight maintenance. VLAN still exists but not allowed on uplink trunk.
final_resolution: Added IoT VLAN to trunk allowed list. IoT devices reconnected. Maintenance runbook updated.

raw_summary: Conference room B4 in Chicago-Loop — users cannot connect to corporate WiFi. Personal hotspots work. Rest of the floor seems fine.
normalized_summary: conference room b4 in chicago-loop — users cannot connect to corporate wifi. personal hotspots work. rest of the floor seems fine.
final_root_cause: Single AP hardware failure. PoE port on switch also degraded.
final_resolution: Replaced AP. Replaced PoE port module. Coverage restored.

raw_summary: Vancouver office — Chime calls dropping every few minutes. Web browsing works. Downloads are slow. Ping to external hosts showing 8% packet loss.
normalized_summary: vancouver office — chime calls dropping every few minutes. web browsing works. downloads are slow. ping to external hosts showing 8% packet loss.
final_root_cause: WAN circuit degraded. Carrier-side issue causing intermittent packet loss on primary path.
final_resolution: Rerouted to secondary circuit. Carrier ticket raised. Primary replaced by carrier next day.

raw_summary: Seattle-Beacon — authentication errors appearing for specific users. Certificate validation failing. Issue started gradually two weeks ago and getting worse.
normalized_summary: seattle-beacon — authentication errors appearing for specific users. certificate validation failing. issue started gradually two weeks ago and getting worse.
final_root_cause: NTP server misconfigured during OS upgrade. Switches drifted 4+ minutes from authoritative time. Certificate time validation failing.
final_resolution: Corrected NTP config. Switches re-synced. Certificate validation restored.

raw_summary: Dublin-Docklands — users connecting to WiFi but getting 169.254.x.x addresses and no network access. Wired users fine. Only started today.
normalized_summary: dublin-docklands — users connecting to wifi but getting 169.254.x.x addresses and no network access. wired users fine. only started today.
final_root_cause: DHCP scope for wireless VLAN exhausted. Large contractor group on-site consuming addresses.
final_resolution: Expanded DHCP scope. Flushed stale leases. Added DHCP scope utilisation monitoring.

raw_summary: Sydney-CBD — wireless is cutting in and out every 10-15 minutes across the whole floor. Wired is fine. APs appear to be rebooting.
normalized_summary: sydney-cbd — wireless is cutting in and out every 10-15 minutes across the whole floor. wired is fine. aps appear to be rebooting.
final_root_cause: Firmware pushed during maintenance window had memory leak bug. APs rebooting every ~12 minutes.
final_resolution: Rolled back firmware to previous version. APs stable. Vendor notified.

raw_summary: Chicago offices — users getting certificate errors when connecting to corporate WiFi. Accepting the cert manually works temporarily but keeps returning.
normalized_summary: chicago offices — users getting certificate errors when connecting to corporate wifi. accepting the cert manually works temporarily but keeps returning.
final_root_cause: RADIUS server certificate expired. No expiry monitoring was in place.
final_resolution: Renewed certificate on ISE. Pushed new cert to all APs. Added cert expiry monitoring with 30-day alert.

raw_summary: Austin office reports issues but our monitoring shows all green. Users say it started an hour ago.
normalized_summary: austin office reports issues but our monitoring shows all green. users say it started an hour ago.
final_root_cause: Monitoring blind spot. Application-layer issue not captured by network monitoring.
final_resolution: On-site investigation found DNS resolution issue affecting specific application. Monitoring gap documented.

raw_summary: Austin-HQ — contractor devices unable to get on the guest network. Employee devices on corporate network are fine. Started this morning.
normalized_summary: austin-hq — contractor devices unable to get on the guest network. employee devices on corporate network are fine. started this morning.
final_root_cause: ISE guest policy updated incorrectly during change window. Contractor device profiling rule broken.
final_resolution: Corrected ISE guest portal policy. Tested with contractor device. Restored access.

raw_summary: Singapore-Marina — engineering team on VLAN 30 cannot reach the print servers on VLAN 40. Other VLANs communicate normally. Issue since this morning.
normalized_summary: singapore-marina — engineering team on vlan 30 cannot reach the print servers on vlan 40. other vlans communicate normally. issue since this morning.
final_root_cause: ACL applied to SVI for VLAN 30 during change window blocking traffic to VLAN 40. ACL rule too broad.
final_resolution: Corrected ACL rule. VLAN 30 to VLAN 40 traffic restored. Change reviewed.

raw_summary: Toronto office — 802.1X errors on all wired ports. Log shows TLS handshake failures. Certificate validity error.
normalized_summary: toronto office — 802.1x errors on all wired ports. log shows tls handshake failures. certificate validity error.
final_root_cause: RADIUS server certificate expired overnight. No monitoring in place.
final_resolution: Emergency cert renewal on ISE. All wired ports authenticating normally. Added cert expiry alert.

raw_summary: Singapore-Marina — all APs showing as disconnected in the management portal. Users still connected but no roaming or management possible. Started 30 minutes ago.
normalized_summary: singapore-marina — all aps showing as disconnected in the management portal. users still connected but no roaming or management possible. started 30 minutes ago.
final_root_cause: Management VLAN routing broken after switch config change. APs still forwarding traffic on data VLAN but control plane to controller lost.
final_resolution: Restored management VLAN routing. Controller regained visibility of all APs.

raw_summary: Bangalore Whitefield completely offline. All users impacted. Building team confirms physical links look fine.
normalized_summary: bangalore whitefield completely offline. all users impacted. building team confirms physical links look fine.
final_root_cause: Carrier circuit failed. No redundant circuit at this site.
final_resolution: Activated emergency 4G link. Escalated to carrier. Documented site as single-circuit risk.

raw_summary: Bangalore-Electronic-City wing B — no WiFi at all. Wing A is fine. Physical AP inspection shows all LEDs are off in wing B.
normalized_summary: bangalore-electronic-city wing b — no wifi at all. wing a is fine. physical ap inspection shows all leds are off in wing b.
final_root_cause: PoE switch powering wing B APs lost power to PoE subsystem after PSU fan failure caused thermal shutdown.
final_resolution: Replaced PSU fan. Switch PoE restored. All APs powered back on.

raw_summary: Chicago-Loop — intermittent outages 30-60 seconds every hour or two. Users report the network just blips. Issue for the past 3 days.
normalized_summary: chicago-loop — intermittent outages 30-60 seconds every hour or two. users report the network just blips. issue for the past 3 days.
final_root_cause: STP topology changes triggered by flapping uplink. Each TCN causing MAC table flush and 30-60 second reconvergence.
final_resolution: Identified flapping uplink. Replaced degraded SFP. Configured STP portfast on stable links.

raw_summary: Austin-Domain large conference room — video calls pixelating and dropping during all-hands meeting. Room has 200 people. Never had issues before with smaller groups.
normalized_summary: austin-domain large conference room — video calls pixelating and dropping during all-hands meeting. room has 200 people. never had issues before with smaller groups.
final_root_cause: AP density insufficient for 200-person high-density event. Channel congestion and airtime exhaustion.
final_resolution: Deployed 4 temporary APs for event. Configured separate SSID with QoS prioritisation for video. Documented high-density event procedure.

raw_summary: Bangalore-Electronic-City — slow internet for two days. Internal resources fast. Only internet-bound traffic affected. Speeds 10% of contracted.
normalized_summary: bangalore-electronic-city — slow internet for two days. internal resources fast. only internet-bound traffic affected. speeds 10% of contracted.
final_root_cause: Last mile ISP throttling. Bandwidth shaping applied incorrectly to circuit after billing dispute.
final_resolution: Escalated to carrier account team. Throttle removed. Full bandwidth restored.

raw_summary: Dublin office users report that applications hosted in us-east-1 are timing out. AWS console is reachable. Direct internet seems fine. Only internal AWS-hosted apps are affected.
normalized_summary: dublin office users report that applications hosted in us-east-1 are timing out. aws console is reachable. direct internet seems fine. only internal aws-hosted apps are affected.
final_root_cause: MPLS path between Dublin and AMER hub degraded. Traffic falling back to public internet path with insufficient QoS.
final_resolution: Engaged carrier. MPLS path restored. QoS policy applied to fallback path.

raw_summary: Dublin-Docklands — specific branch office cannot reach HQ after route policy change yesterday. All other branches fine.
normalized_summary: dublin-docklands — specific branch office cannot reach hq after route policy change yesterday. all other branches fine.
final_root_cause: Route policy change denied prefix for one branch office CIDR. Route map regex too greedy.
final_resolution: Corrected route map regex. Branch prefix re-advertised. Connectivity restored.

raw_summary: New-York-Brooklyn — executives on floor 6 losing calls when moving between south and north wings. Both wings on same floor, same SSID.
normalized_summary: new-york-brooklyn — executives on floor 6 losing calls when moving between south and north wings. both wings on same floor, same ssid.
final_root_cause: AP group boundary running through middle of floor. Roaming handoff not configured across groups.
final_resolution: Consolidated floor 6 into single AP group. Roaming now seamless.

raw_summary: Vancouver-Gastown — after IOS-XE upgrade on core switch last night, random users experiencing network instability. Not consistent pattern.
normalized_summary: vancouver-gastown — after ios-xe upgrade on core switch last night, random users experiencing network instability. not consistent pattern.
final_root_cause: IOS-XE version 17.9.1 has known TCAM resource exhaustion bug. Manifests as random forwarding failures.
final_resolution: Emergency rollback to 17.6.3. Stability restored. Vendor opened TAC case.

raw_summary: Vancouver-Gastown — specific internal service (HR portal) unreachable from office. Other internal services fine. Service team confirms server is healthy.
normalized_summary: vancouver-gastown — specific internal service (hr portal) unreachable from office. other internal services fine. service team confirms server is healthy.
final_root_cause: Route to HR portal subnet missing from office edge router after route policy change removed the prefix.
final_resolution: Corrected route policy. Prefix re-advertised. HR portal accessible. Route policy change reviewed.

raw_summary: User in Austin says their internet is not working. Everyone else seems fine.
normalized_summary: user in austin says their internet is not working. everyone else seems fine.
final_root_cause: Single user issue — cable unplugged. Not a network incident.
final_resolution: On-site IT verified physical cable. Reconnected. Issue resolved.

raw_summary: Singapore-Marina — WAN interface showing as up/down in monitoring every few minutes. Users experiencing connectivity drops.
normalized_summary: singapore-marina — wan interface showing as up/down in monitoring every few minutes. users experiencing connectivity drops.
final_root_cause: Fiber cable partially seated on carrier hand-off. Vibration from HVAC causing intermittent disconnects.
final_resolution: Carrier dispatched. Reseated fiber connector. Cleaned and tested. Interface stable.
